content: RequestAnimationFrame vs CSS transitions for combat animations: For an idle RPG with turn-based combat (1.5s poll interval), CSS transitions/animations are the better choice for most effects. Rationale: (1) CSS animations are GPU-accelerated (transform, opacity, box-shadow) and don't block the JS thread. (2) rAF is better for continuous/animated content (60fps loops, canvas games, physics). Idle RPG combat is discrete events separated by pauses — CSS handles this natively with animationstart/animationend events. (3) Where rAF wins: projectile motion that needs curved trajectories (arc arrows, lobbed projectiles) and particle systems (spark bursts on death). These need frame-by-frame position calculation. (4) Best practice: Hybrid approach — use CSS animations for 80% of effects (lunge, flash, shake, fade, bounce) and rAF for the 20% that needs frame-level control (projectile arcs, particle explosions). The CSS animations handle the class-toggle lifecycle; the rAF animations manually set style.left/style.top.
